text:
BOTTOM LINE — Small caps outperform amid Iran tensions, tech sells hard. Markets split sharply: small-cap resilience (+0.57% IWM) clashed with Nasdaq weakness as geopolitical risk spiked oil volatility while investors fled growth and financials into defensive trades. Peace deal uncertainty fueled rotation rather than capitulation—breadth divergence signals tactical repositioning, not systemic breakdown.

INDICES — VIX at 18.9, below 20d MA of 23.0, signaling contained fear despite headlines. Small-cap winners: IWM +0.57%, IJH +0.63%, IJR +0.56%; large-cap laggards: QQQ -0.32%, SPY -0.20%, DIA +0.02%. Small > large by 0.77pp. Most notable cross-asset move: USO -7.79% despite Iran tensions—oil futures collapsed as ceasefire doubts eased supply premium. SPY and QQQ both above 200d MAs; no CTA trigger.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.5x (below average), QQQ vol_ratio 0.6x (elevated relative to SPY). Gainers dominated breadth: GLD 0.7x, DIA 0.6x rising on modest volume—distribution signal into safe havens. XLY 1.0x and XLF 0.9x accumulating on sector strength; XLV 0.5x and XLC 0.5x withering. Low-conviction rally in large caps masked by sector rotation into cyclicals.

SECTORS — XLY Discretionary +2.36%, XLI Industrials +1.87%, XLK Technology +1.53%, XLRE Real Estate +1.53%, XLP Staples +1.26%, XLF Financials +0.77%, XLB Materials +0.25%, XLE Energy +0.09%, XLC Comms -0.29%, XLU Utilities -0.89%, XLV Healthcare -0.93%. Top two: Discretionary and Industrials. Bottom two: Utilities and Healthcare.

THE 7s — Top 5: Software 7 +2.01%, Freight 7 +1.50%, Industrial 7 +1.35%, Buildout 7 +1.11%, New Metals 7 +0.95%. Bottom 5: Energy 7 -2.21%, Mag 7 -0.95%, Power 7 -0.95%, Old Metals 7 -1.15%, New Staples 7 -0.82%. Software 7 strongest ticker: undisclosed. Energy 7 weakest: undisclosed. Old Metals 7 shows internal divergence—gold down 0.86% while silver down 2.01%—demand destruction in industrial metals vs safe-haven bid.

FED & LIQUIDITY — FOMC April 29; CME probabilities: hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%. Fed tally: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves—hawkish lean with Cook, Hammack, Kashkari vocal. MOVE index at 65.7, well below 20d MA of 85.4—bond volatility compressed despite headlines, suggesting fixed-income stability.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR 1.28 (elevated protection demand), QQQ PCR 2.71 (high put buying). Skew positive across both: SPY +6.7%, QQQ +7.3%—fear premium intact. GEX flip SPY 715 vs spot near 701—dealer hedging near inflection; small moves could amplify. VIX 18.9 vs 20d MA 23.0 signals complacency despite geopolitical headlines; VVIX at 98.2 below 20d MA 111.9 confirms volatility compression.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil-equity transmission break: USO -7.79% while XLE +0.09%—energy stocks decoupled from crude as margin expectations shifted. Gold -0.86% despite risk-off posture unusual; silver's -2.01% collapse signals industrial demand destruction over safe-haven bid amid Iran uncertainty.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 56.9/100 (neutral). Top 3 movers: MSOS +8.75% (Cannabis, 3.1x vol spike—accumulation), USO -7.79% (Energy ETF, structural demand reset), DBC -2.82% (Commodities, 2.5x vol). 20d momentum: best sector XLK +11.64%, worst XLU +3.18%; best basket Semi 7 +33.04%, worst Energy 7 -4.60%. Iran ceasefire doubts drove oil volatility spike but failed to propagate broad risk-off.

WATCH — (1) MSOS +8.75%, Cannabis, 3.1x volume spike with price-vol divergence suggesting accumulation into regulation hopes. (2) USO -7.79%, Oil, structural breakdown despite geopolitical premium—watch for supply narrative reset. (3) XLY +2.36%, Discretionary, momentum divergence against tech weakness signals consumer resilience narrative shift.
